Title: Process ADT^A04 (Register a Patient) and create new patient record
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface engine listening on MLLP port; patient does not yet exist
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with MSH, EVN, PID, PV1 | 2. Observe interface engine parse and route | 3. Verify patient created in registry | 4. Confirm ACK returned
Expected_Result: New patient created with demographics from PID; AA acknowledgment returned; encounter NOT auto-discharged

Title: Process ADT^A08 (Update Patient Information) and merge demographic changes
Preconditions: Existing patient with MRN matching PID-3 present in system
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 with changed PID-5 surname and PID-11 address | 2. Verify interface applies update | 3. Confirm existing identifiers retained | 4. Confirm ACK AA
Expected_Result: Existing patient demographics updated in place; no duplicate created; AA returned

Title: Process ADT^A40 (Merge Patient) using MRG segment to consolidate duplicate records
Preconditions: Two patient records exist: surviving MRN and prior MRN to be merged
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A40 with PID surviving MRN and MRG prior MRN | 2. Verify interface merges records | 3. Confirm clinical data reassigned to survivor | 4. Confirm prior MRN marked merged
Expected_Result: Records merged; encounters/obs from MRN200 reassigned to MRN100; MRN200 deactivated; AA returned

Title: Reject ADT^A40 merge when MRG-1 identifier does not exist
Preconditions: Surviving patient exists; prior MRN in MRG does not exist
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A40 with MRG-1 referencing nonexistent MRN | 2. Observe interface validation | 3. Confirm no merge performed | 4. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Merge not performed; AE application error returned with reason; survivor untouched

Title: Process ADT^A02 (Transfer a Patient) and update assigned location
Preconditions: Patient has active admitted visit at ward A
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A02 with new PV1-3 location | 2. Verify location transition applied | 3. Confirm prior location closed | 4. Confirm ACK AA
Expected_Result: Patient location updated to WARDB; visit remains active; transfer event recorded; AA returned

Title: Process ADT^A03 (Discharge/End Visit) and close active visit
Preconditions: Patient has an active inpatient visit
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A03 with PV1-45 discharge datetime | 2. Verify visit transitions to discharged | 3. Confirm stop datetime set | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Active visit closed with discharge datetime; disposition recorded; subsequent A03 for same visit is idempotent or AE

Title: Process ADT^A11 (Cancel Admit) reversing a prior admission
Preconditions: Patient was admitted via prior ADT^A01/A04 with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A11 referencing the admission | 2. Verify admission cancelled | 3. Confirm visit removed or voided | 4. Confirm ACK AA
Expected_Result: Admission cancelled; visit voided not discharged; no residual active encounter; AA returned

Title: Process ADT^A12 (Cancel Transfer) restoring prior location
Preconditions: Patient was transferred via prior A02 to WARDB
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A12 | 2. Verify location reverts to pre-transfer WARDA | 3. Confirm transfer event voided | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Location reverts to WARDA; transfer record voided; visit stays active; AA returned

Title: Process ADT^A13 (Cancel Discharge) reactivating a discharged visit
Preconditions: Patient visit previously discharged via A03
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A13 referencing discharged visit | 2. Verify visit reopened | 3. Confirm discharge datetime cleared | 4. Confirm ACK AA
Expected_Result: Visit reactivated; stop datetime cleared; patient shows active again; AA returned

Title: Process ADT^A05 (Pre-Admit a Patient) creating a pending visit
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A05 with PV1-2=P pre-admit class | 2. Verify pre-admit visit created | 3. Confirm not counted as admitted census | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Pre-admit visit created in pending state; not in active census; AA returned

Title: Reject message with missing MSH-9 message type
Preconditions: Interface engine active with schema validation
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT message with MSH-9 empty | 2. Observe parser validation | 3. Confirm message rejected | 4. Inspect ACK code
Expected_Result: Message rejected; AR reject ACK returned with MSA-3 citing required MSH-9 missing

Title: Validate MSH-1 field separator and MSH-2 encoding characters parsing
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with standard MSH-1=pipe and MSH-2=^~\\& | 2. Verify component, repetition, escape, subcomponent delimiters honored | 3. Parse a repeating PID-13 | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Encoding chars applied; repetitions and components split correctly; AA returned

Title: Handle non-standard custom encoding characters in MSH-2
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with MSH-2 set to alternate delimiters | 2. Verify parser dynamically uses declared delimiters | 3. Parse components/subcomponents | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Parser uses declared delimiters from MSH-2 not hardcoded; fields parse correctly; AA returned

Title: Reject message when MSH-12 version id is unsupported
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with MSH-12=2.1 | 2. Observe version negotiation | 3. Confirm handling per config | 4. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Either AE returned citing unsupported version, or downgrade handled per config; behavior consistent and logged

Title: Generate AA (Application Accept) for valid processed message
Test_Steps: 1. Send valid ADT^A04 | 2. Observe ACK | 3. Verify MSA-1=AA | 4. Verify MSA-2 echoes MSH-10 control id
Expected_Result: ACK^A04 returned; MSA-1=AA; MSA-2=MSGCTRL001; MSH-9 of ack is ACK

Title: Generate AE (Application Error) for message failing business validation
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with PID-5 patient name empty | 2. Observe validation failure | 3. Verify MSA-1=AE | 4. Verify error detail in ERR segment
Expected_Result: ACK with MSA-1=AE; ERR segment populated with location and code; message not persisted

Title: Generate AR (Application Reject) for malformed unparseable message
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with corrupted segment structure missing MSH | 2. Observe parse failure | 3. Verify MSA-1=AR | 4. Confirm no processing attempted
Expected_Result: ACK with MSA-1=AR returned; message rejected at parse layer; nothing persisted; error logged

Title: Validate enhanced acknowledgment mode using MSH-15 and MSH-16
Test_Steps: 1. Send messages varying MSH-15 (AL/NE/ER/SU) and MSH-16 | 2. Observe commit ack vs application ack | 3. Verify ack returned only per rule | 4. Confirm correctness
Expected_Result: Acks generated per enhanced-mode decision table: AL=always, NE=never, ER=on error, SU=on success; commit vs app ack separated

Title: Validate PID-7 date of birth datatype TS format boundaries
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-7 with YYYYMMDD only | 2. Send PID-7 with full TS YYYYMMDDHHMMSS | 3. Send PID-7 with invalid month 13 | 4. Observe handling
Expected_Result: Valid precisions accepted; invalid month 13 rejected with AE citing PID-7 datatype error

Title: Validate PID-8 administrative sex coded value against table 0001
Preconditions: Interface uses HL7 table 0001 for sex
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-8=M | 2. Send F | 3. Send O | 4. Send U | 5. Send invalid X
Expected_Result: M/F/O/U/A/N accepted and mapped; invalid X rejected or mapped to unknown per config; consistent

Title: Validate PID-3 repeating patient identifier list with assigning authority
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-3 with repeating CX identifiers MRN and SSN | 2. Verify each parsed with type code CX-5 | 3. Confirm assigning authority CX-4 retained | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Both identifiers ingested with correct type and assigning authority; AA returned

Title: Reject ADT when PID-3 required MRN identifier is absent
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 without PID-3 | 2. Observe required field validation | 3. Confirm reject | 4. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Message rejected AE; ERR cites PID-3 required; no orphan patient created

Title: Handle PID-5 name with multiple components family given middle suffix prefix
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-5 with all XPN components | 2. Verify family/given/middle/suffix/prefix mapped | 3. Confirm name type code | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Name components mapped correctly including suffix and prefix; name type L legal honored; AA returned

Title: Validate PID-11 patient address repeating with address type codes
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-11 with home and mailing repeats varying country/state | 2. Verify both parsed | 3. Confirm address type XAD-7 | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Multiple addresses parsed with type codes H and M; pairwise state/country combos handled; AA returned

Title: Validate EVN-2 recorded datetime and EVN-6 event occurred datetime
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT with EVN-2 and EVN-6 differing | 2. Verify both captured | 3. Confirm event timestamp used for clinical timing | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: EVN-6 used as clinical event time; EVN-2 as record time; both retained; AA returned

Title: Validate EVN-1 event type code consistency with MSH-9 trigger
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with EVN-1=A08 mismatch | 2. Observe consistency check | 3. Confirm handling | 4. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Mismatch detected; either AE returned or MSH-9 authoritative per config; behavior logged and consistent

Title: Validate PV1-2 patient class coded values I O E P R B
Test_Steps: 1. Send messages with PV1-2 each class value | 2. Verify mapping to visit type | 3. Confirm invalid class rejected | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: I/O/E/P/R/B mapped to visit types; invalid Z rejected or defaulted per config; consistent

Title: Validate PV1-7 attending provider parsed with role assignment
Test_Steps: 1. Send PV1-7 attending provider XCN | 2. Verify provider matched in directory | 3. Confirm role attending assigned to encounter | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Attending provider matched and linked with attending role; AA returned

Title: Handle PV1-19 visit number used for encounter correlation across messages
Preconditions: Interface correlates messages by visit number
Test_Steps: 1. Send A04 with PV1-19=VISIT777 | 2. Send A02 same PV1-19 | 3. Send A03 same PV1-19 | 4. Verify all applied to one visit
Expected_Result: All three messages correlate to single visit lifecycle; no duplicate visits; final state discharged

Title: Process ORM^O01 new order with ORC-1=NW creating an order
Preconditions: Order interface active; orderable catalog mapped
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORM^O01 with ORC-1=NW and OBR | 2. Verify order created | 3. Confirm ORC-2 placer order number stored | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: New order created with placer order number; status active; AA returned

Title: Process ORC-1=CA cancel order against existing placer order
Preconditions: An active order with placer order number exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORM with ORC-1=CA referencing placer number | 2. Verify order cancelled | 3. Confirm status transition | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Existing order transitions to cancelled; AA returned; cancel of nonexistent order yields AE

Title: Validate ORC-5 order status and ORC-9 transaction datetime
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORC with status values SC IP CM CA DC | 2. Verify status mapping | 3. Confirm invalid status handled | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Valid statuses mapped per table 0038; invalid XX rejected or defaulted; ORC-9 datetime captured

Title: Process ORU^R01 unsolicited observation result with OBR and OBX
Preconditions: Result interface active; patient and order exist
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with OBR filler order and OBX results | 2. Verify results filed to order | 3. Confirm OBX values and units | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Results filed against matching order; OBX value 95 mg/dL recorded; AA returned

Title: Validate OBX-2 value type drives OBX-5 datatype parsing
Test_Steps: 1. Send OBX-2=NM numeric value | 2. Send OBX-2=ST string | 3. Send OBX-2=CE coded | 4. Send OBX-2=TX text | 5. Verify parse per type
Expected_Result: Each value type parsed per its datatype; NM validated numeric; CE split into code/text/system; AA returned

Title: Reject OBX-5 non-numeric value when OBX-2 declares NM
Test_Steps: 1. Send OBX-2=NM with OBX-5=ABC | 2. Observe datatype validation | 3. Confirm reject | 4. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Datatype mismatch detected; AE returned with ERR citing OBX-5; value not filed

Title: Validate OBX-8 abnormal flags and OBX-11 result status interpretation
Test_Steps: 1. Send OBX-8=H high | 2. Send OBX-8=L low | 3. Send OBX-8=HH critical high | 4. Vary OBX-11 P/F/C/X | 5. Verify mapping
Expected_Result: Abnormal flags mapped to interpretation per table 0078; result status preliminary/final/corrected mapped per 0085; AA returned

Title: Process corrected result OBX-11=C overwriting prior preliminary result
Preconditions: Prior preliminary result filed for same OBX-3
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU with OBX-11=C and changed OBX-5 | 2. Verify correction supersedes prior | 3. Confirm prior retained in history | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Corrected value 105 becomes current; prior 95 retained as superseded history; correction audited; AA returned

Title: Validate OBR-4 universal service identifier mapping to local catalog
Preconditions: Service catalog mapping configured for LOINC
Test_Steps: 1. Send OBR-4 with code^text^coding-system | 2. Verify mapped to local orderable | 3. Confirm unmapped code handling | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: OBR-4 LOINC code mapped to local service; unmapped code flagged for review; AA or AE per config

Title: Validate OBR-7 observation datetime and OBR-22 result report status datetime
Test_Steps: 1. Send OBR-7 collection datetime | 2. Send OBR-22 report datetime later | 3. Verify temporal ordering | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Collection time precedes report time; both stored; future-dated collection flagged; AA returned

Title: Process SIU^S12 (New Appointment Booking) creating scheduled slot
Preconditions: Scheduling interface active; provider schedule exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S12 with SCH and AIS/AIL/AIP/AIG | 2. Verify appointment created | 3. Confirm SCH-1 placer appt id stored | 4. Confirm ACK
Test_Data: SCH-1=APPT001; AIS service; AIL location; AIP provider
Expected_Result: Appointment created with placer id; provider/location/service linked; AA returned

Title: Process SIU^S14 (Appointment Modification) updating existing slot
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S14 with changed AIS start time | 2. Verify appointment updated | 3. Confirm no duplicate | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Existing appointment rescheduled; single record updated; AA returned

Title: Process SIU^S15 (Appointment Cancellation) cancelling a booked slot
Test_Steps: 1. Send SIU^S15 referencing placer id | 2. Verify appointment cancelled | 3. Confirm slot released | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Appointment transitions to cancelled; slot freed; AA returned; cancel of unknown id yields AE

Title: Process MDM^T02 (Original Document Notification and Content)
Test_Steps: 1. Send MDM^T02 with TXA and OBX document content | 2. Verify document stored | 3. Confirm TXA-12 unique doc number | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Document created and linked to patient/encounter; doc number stored; content retained; AA returned

Title: Process MDM^T06 (Document Addendum) appending to existing document
Test_Steps: 1. Send MDM^T06 referencing parent TXA-12 | 2. Verify addendum linked to original | 3. Confirm original not overwritten | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Addendum stored and linked to original document; original preserved; document version chain intact; AA returned

Title: Validate TXA-19 document availability and TXA-17 document completion status
Test_Steps: 1. Send TXA-17 status DI/DO/IP/IN/PA | 2. Send TXA-19 AV/CA/OB/UN | 3. Verify mapping | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Completion and availability statuses mapped per tables 0271/0273; invalid combos flagged; AA returned

Title: Process DFT^P03 (Post Detail Financial Transaction) creating charge
Preconditions: Billing interface active; patient with visit exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send DFT^P03 with FT1 charge detail | 2. Verify charge posted | 3. Confirm FT1-7 transaction code mapped | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Charge of 150.00 posted to patient account; transaction code mapped; AA returned

Title: Validate DFT FT1-6 transaction type and FT1-11 amount datatype MO
Test_Steps: 1. Send FT1-11 with positive amount | 2. Send negative credit amount | 3. Send zero | 4. Send malformed currency | 5. Verify handling
Expected_Result: Valid money amounts posted; negative as credit; malformed rejected AE; AA for valid

Title: Process batch with BHS/BTS wrapping multiple messages
Test_Steps: 1. Send batch file BHS then 3 ADT messages then BTS | 2. Verify each message processed | 3. Confirm BTS-1 batch message count matches | 4. Confirm batch ack
Expected_Result: All 3 messages processed; BTS count validated against actual; batch acknowledgment returned

Title: Reject batch when BTS-1 declared count mismatches actual message count
Test_Steps: 1. Send batch with BTS-1=3 but only 2 messages present | 2. Observe count validation | 3. Confirm batch handling | 4. Inspect ack
Expected_Result: Count mismatch detected; batch flagged with error; processed messages tracked; error logged

Title: Validate FHS/FTS file header trailer wrapping multiple batches
Test_Steps: 1. Send file with FHS, two batches each BHS/BTS, FTS | 2. Verify all batches processed | 3. Confirm FTS-1 batch count | 4. Confirm acks
Expected_Result: File and both batches processed; FTS batch count validated; per-batch acks generated

Title: Detect and reject duplicate message by MSH-10 control id
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 with MSH-10=DUP001 | 2. Resend identical MSH-10=DUP001 | 3. Verify second detected as duplicate | 4. Inspect handling
Expected_Result: First processed AA; duplicate detected and not reprocessed; idempotent; logged as duplicate

Title: Validate MSH-13 sequence number gap detection and out-of-order handling
Preconditions: Interface configured for sequence number protocol
Test_Steps: 1. Send messages with MSH-13=1,2,then 5 | 2. Observe gap detection | 3. Confirm out-of-order handling | 4. Inspect ack
Expected_Result: Sequence gap detected; interface requests retransmit or holds per config; ordering integrity preserved

Title: Process custom Z-segment ZPD without rejecting standard message
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 containing a ZPD custom segment | 2. Verify standard segments still processed | 3. Confirm Z-segment handled or ignored per config | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Standard PID/PV1 processed normally; ZPD ignored or mapped per config; message not rejected; AA returned

Title: Validate configured Z-segment field mapping into custom attribute
Preconditions: Interface configured to map ZPD-2 to attribute
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with ZPD-2 populated | 2. Verify mapped to configured attribute | 3. Confirm value persisted | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: ZPD-2 value mapped and persisted to configured custom attribute; AA returned

Title: Validate HL7 escape sequences for delimiter chars within field data
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-5 containing escaped pipe \\F\\ and ampersand \\T\\ | 2. Verify unescaped to literal | 3. Confirm field not split | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Escape sequences \\F\\ \\S\\ \\T\\ \\R\\ \\E\\ resolved to literal delimiters; field integrity preserved; AA returned

Title: Validate MSH-18 character set handling for non-ASCII names
Test_Steps: 1. Send MSH-18=UNICODE UTF-8 with accented name | 2. Verify characters preserved | 3. Confirm no mojibake | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Non-ASCII characters preserved through ingestion and storage; no corruption; AA returned

Title: Validate MLLP framing start block end block carriage return enforcement
Test_Steps: 1. Send message missing MLLP end-block bytes | 2. Observe framing handling | 3. Confirm message not prematurely processed | 4. Verify timeout/reject
Expected_Result: Incomplete frame not processed; listener waits or times out; partial frame discarded; logged

Title: Reject HL7 message over unencrypted channel when TLS required
Preconditions: Interface policy requires MLLP over TLS
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt plaintext MLLP connection | 2. Observe connection handling | 3. Confirm rejection | 4. Verify audit log
Expected_Result: Plaintext connection refused; only TLS handshake accepted; rejection audited

Title: Validate HL7 ingestion sanitizes injection payloads in free-text fields
Preconditions: Interface active; downstream uses field values
Test_Steps: 1. Send OBX-5 containing script/SQL-like payload | 2. Verify stored as literal text | 3. Confirm no injection downstream | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Payload stored and rendered as inert literal text; no script execution or SQL injection; AA returned

Title: Validate every inbound HL7 message and its ACK are persisted to audit log
Test_Steps: 1. Send several ADT/ORU messages | 2. Query message archive table | 3. Verify raw payload, control id, ack code stored | 4. Confirm timestamps
Expected_Result: Each message persisted with raw payload, MSH-10, ack result, received and processed timestamps; immutable audit trail

Title: Validate ADT^A04 ingestion produces equivalent FHIR Patient via adapter
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer maps HL7 to FHIR; HAPI FHIR target
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A04 | 2. Query FHIR Patient by identifier | 3. Verify name/gender/birthDate parity | 4. Confirm identifier system
Expected_Result: FHIR Patient created with matching identifier, name, gender male, birthDate; HL7-to-FHIR field parity preserved

Title: Validate ORU^R01 result maps to FHIR Observation with correct code system
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer maps OBX to FHIR Observation
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU with OBX LOINC coded result | 2. Query FHIR Observation | 3. Verify code.system is LOINC URL | 4. Confirm value and unit
Expected_Result: FHIR Observation created with code.system http://loinc.org, value 95, unit mg/dL; status mapped from OBX-11

Title: Route unprocessable message to error queue without dropping payload
Test_Steps: 1. Send message that fails processing | 2. Verify routed to error queue | 3. Confirm payload retained for reprocessing | 4. Verify alert raised
Expected_Result: Failed message moved to error queue with full payload and reason; not silently dropped; alert/notification raised

Title: Explore handling of extremely large ORU with hundreds of OBX repeats
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU containing 500 OBX segments | 2. Observe parse and persistence | 3. Monitor memory/time | 4. Confirm completeness
Test_Data: Single ORU with 500 OBX result lines
Expected_Result: All OBX results filed without truncation or timeout; performance acceptable; no partial-commit; AA returned

Title: Validate field length boundary for max-length PID-5 family name
Test_Steps: 1. Send PID-5 family name at datatype max length | 2. Send exceeding length | 3. Observe truncation/reject | 4. Confirm ACK
Expected_Result: Max-length accepted; over-length truncated to limit or rejected per config consistently; behavior documented

Title: Pairwise combinations of message type, ack mode, and TLS for routing
Preconditions: Interface supports multiple message types and modes
Test_Steps: 1. Vary message type ADT/ORU/ORM | 2. Vary ack mode original/enhanced | 3. Vary transport TLS/plaintext-allowed | 4. Verify correct routing/ack per combo
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination routes correctly and returns appropriate ack; no combination crashes or misroutes
